name: What Section Seven Says
description: Solen has spent three years in the sunken city archive concluding that the SCAMP device used the Grid's own construction wave with the polarity inverted. He needs a second reader. Go to the archive and read section seven of the resonance engineering records.
accept_text: He shows you the notebook. The handwriting is small because he has been conservative with paper — three-quarters full after three years, the last pages denser than the first. 'The archive is north.' He points. 'The indexing is relational, not hierarchical. The Elder People organized records by what they share with other records, not by subject or date. Start with the resonance engineering section — the symbol cluster that looks like a wave intersecting a lattice grid. It will find you, once you're in the right section.' He opens the notebook to a specific page. 'This is what I've concluded from three years of reading. The SCAMP device uses the same frequency base as the Grid's construction wave — the pulse the Elder People used to build the lattice, node by node, across the Wastes. The mechanism is the same. The polarity is inverted.' He taps the page. 'Inversion converts a construction wave into a disruption wave. The construction wave builds resonance, connects nodes, extends the network outward. Inverted: it disrupts resonance, severs connections, propagates the severing. The Cascade was not a power accident. The Cascade was the Grid's own construction wave, running backwards, using the lattice's own propagation mechanism to amplify the destruction.' He is quiet. 'Every node, turning against every other node. Using the same self-sustaining propagation that had been building the network for three hundred years before the Elder People completed it.' He looks at you. 'I need you to go to the archive and read the original construction specification. Section seven. Tell me if you read the same thing I read. Because if you do —' He stops. 'Because if you do, the weapon that ended the Elder People's civilization was built from their own most fundamental technology. And whoever built it understood the Grid deeply enough to reverse-engineer a weapon from its core mechanism. That is not an accident. That is a choice.'
complete_text: The archive is organized exactly as Solen described, and once the indexing structure becomes clear the path to the resonance engineering section is direct. Section seven is exactly where he said. The original construction specification is illustrated — resonance diagrams, frequency geometry, the mathematics of a wave propagating through the lattice from node to node, each node amplifying and extending the propagation to the next. The notation is Elder-native but legible. You read it twice. A third time. Then you sit in Solen's chair — the camping equipment is arranged with the orderliness of a man who has been here long enough to become comfortable, to know where things are without looking — and you stay. The engineering is elegant the way things that work are elegant, the elegance being evidence of function. The construction wave builds by resonating at the lattice's natural frequency and letting the lattice amplify the resonance outward. Self-sustaining, once initiated. On the third reading you understand what Solen understood: a wave this structural, propagating through infrastructure this complete, inverted and run backwards, would not merely damage the lattice. It would use the lattice to amplify its own destruction. Every node transmitting disruption to every other node, with the same self-sustaining propagation that had been building the network. The Cascade had three hundred years of network to work through. It used all of it. You go back to Solen.
